berlin.golden_bear.note: Paul Thomas Anderson's Golden Bear for Magnolia — PT Anderson's only major-festival top prize and the most-celebrated American Golden Bear of the early 2000s. Anderson returned to Berlin's prize roster eight years later (cer 2008 Best Director Silver Bear for There Will Be Blood) — same-director cross-decade Golden Bear + Silver Bear pair. Magnolia is one of the canonical late-1990s American auteur works.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 50th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Chinese director Zhang Yimou's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for The Road Home — minimalist rural-romance flashback film featuring an early-career Zhang Ziyi. Zhang Yimou is one of Berlin's most-decorated repeat laureates of the 1980s-90s; cer 2000 is his Silver Bear top-up after his earlier Golden Bear wins (Red Sorghum 1988, etc.).
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 50th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Czech-American veteran Miloš Forman (One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest, Amadeus) takes the Silver Bear for Best Director for Man on the Moon — Andy Kaufman biopic starring Jim Carrey. Forman's last major-festival prize before his 2018 death.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 50th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Denzel Washington's Silver Bear for Best Actor for Norman Jewison's The Hurricane — biographical drama on Rubin 'Hurricane' Carter. Washington was nominated for the 2000 Best Actor Oscar for the same role; cer 2000 Berlin is one of his major non-Hollywood-academy honours.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 50th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: TIE for Silver Bear Best Actress: Bibiana Beglau AND Nadja Uhl, both for the same film — Volker Schlöndorff's The Legend of Rita / Die Stille nach dem Schuss (RAF / East-German political thriller). Scraper produces 2 winner rows (split_co_recipients=true on this category). Same-film Best Actress TIE — extremely rare cross-acting double on a single film.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 50th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Japanese director Akira Ogata — Alfred Bauer for the school-set drama Boy's Choir.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 50th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2000.notes: 50th Berlinale (anniversary edition) — Paul Thomas Anderson's only major-festival Golden Bear (Magnolia); same-film Best Actress TIE (Beglau + Uhl for The Legend of Rita). Outstanding Artistic Contribution was awarded to 'The Cast of Paradiso: Seven Days with Seven Women' (ensemble citation, no individual recipient) — scraper correctly skips the row because the recipient is filtered as a non-person label. Best Screenplay and Best First Feature did not exist as Silver Bear categories at this Berlinale (Best Screenplay debuted cer 2008, Best First Feature debuted cer 2006); first_year filters in expectations correctly skip both.

berlin.golden_bear.note: French theatre+film director Patrice Chéreau's Golden Bear for Intimacy — explicit-sex London-set chamber drama based on Hanif Kureishi short stories. Same-film cross-honour: Intimacy also took Best Actress (Kerry Fox) at the same ceremony — Golden Bear + Best Actress double. Chéreau later won Berlin Best Director two years on (cer 2003, His Brother) — Berlin Golden Bear → Best Director cross-ceremony pair.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 51st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Chinese director Wang Xiaoshuai's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for Beijing Bicycle — youth-class drama recalling De Sica's Bicycle Thieves. Wang Xiaoshuai later took the inaugural Berlin Best Screenplay (cer 2008, In Love We Trust) and same-film Best Actor + Actress double (cer 2019, So Long, My Son) — three-prize Berlin career arc.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 51st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Taiwanese director Lin Cheng-sheng — youth-crime drama set in Taipei.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 51st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Benicio del Toro's Silver Bear for Best Actor for Steven Soderbergh's Traffic — for which he also won the 2001 Best Supporting Actor Oscar at the same year's Academy Awards. Cer 2001 Berlin is one of two major-festival honours for the same role.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 51st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: New Zealand actress Kerry Fox wins for Intimacy — same-film Golden Bear + Best Actress double with director Patrice Chéreau at the same ceremony.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 51st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Spanish DP Raúl Pérez Cubero — implicit cinematography citation (no specialty parenthetical in the wikitext bullet, but Pérez Cubero is a long-time DP).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 51st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Argentine director Lucrecia Martel's debut feature La Ciénaga takes the Alfred Bauer Prize — pre-figures Martel's emergence as one of the most-celebrated Latin American auteurs of the 21st century (Zama, etc.). Cer 2001 is her career-launching festival prize.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 51st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2001.notes: 51st Berlinale — Intimacy (Patrice Chéreau) takes a same-film Golden Bear + Best Actress double (Kerry Fox); Wang Xiaoshuai's Grand Jury win launches his three-prize Berlin career arc; Lucrecia Martel's debut launches her major auteur career. Best Screenplay and Best First Feature did not exist as Silver Bear categories (lifecycle absence).

berlin.golden_bear.note: TIE for Golden Bear: Paul Greengrass for Bloody Sunday (Northern-Ireland-1972 docu-drama) AND Hayao Miyazaki for Spirited Away (Japanese animation). Scraper produces 2 winner rows (Bloody Sunday first, Spirited Away second per Wikipedia bullet order). The Miyazaki side is a permanent festival landmark — FIRST animated film to win the Berlin Golden Bear, and Miyazaki's only major Western festival top prize. Spirited Away went on to win the 2003 Best Animated Feature Oscar. Greengrass's Bloody Sunday side is itself notable as one of the most-acclaimed political-cinema works of the early 2000s. Same-ceremony Golden Bear TIE between live-action political drama and hand-drawn animation is unprecedented.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 52nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: German director Andreas Dresen — relationship drama Halbe Treppe / Grill Point.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 52nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Georgian-French veteran Otar Iosseliani — Silver Bear for Best Director for Monday Morning. Iosseliani is one of the most-celebrated post-Soviet émigré directors; cer 2002 Berlin is his major-festival peak.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 52nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: French actor Jacques Gamblin — Bertrand Tavernier's Vichy-era drama.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 52nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Halle Berry's Silver Bear for Best Actress for Monster's Ball — at the same year's Academy Awards she became the FIRST Black woman to win the Best Actress Oscar (also for Monster's Ball). Cer 2002 Berlin's recognition is therefore part of one of the most-defining acting-prize doubles of the early 2000s.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 52nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded to the 'Ensemble of actresses' of François Ozon's 8 Women / 8 femmes — Catherine Deneuve, Isabelle Huppert, Emmanuelle Béart, Fanny Ardant, Virginie Ledoyen, Danielle Darrieux, Ludivine Sagnier, Firmine Richard. Scraper extracts the literal phrase 'Ensemble of actresses' as the recipient since no individual names appear in the wikitext bullet. The eight Frenchwomen ensemble represents one of the strongest single-film acting collectives of the 2000s.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 52nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: German director Christopher Roth — Andreas Baader / RAF biopic.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 52nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2002.notes: 52nd Berlinale — TIE for Golden Bear: Bloody Sunday (Paul Greengrass) + Spirited Away (Hayao Miyazaki). FIRST animated film to win the Berlin Golden Bear (Miyazaki, also went on to a Best Animated Feature Oscar). Halle Berry takes Best Actress for Monster's Ball — the same role that won her the historic 2002 Best Actress Oscar. OAC awarded to the eight-actress ensemble of 8 Women (no individual names; scraper extracts 'Ensemble of actresses' as recipient). Best Screenplay and BFF lifecycle-absent.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Michael Winterbottom's Golden Bear for In This World — Afghan-refugee-journey docu-drama. Winterbottom returned to Berlin's prize roster three years later (cer 2006 Best Director TIE for The Road to Guantanamo) — Golden Bear → Best Director cross-ceremony pair within four years.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 53rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Spike Jonze's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for Adaptation — Charlie Kaufman meta-screenplay. Jonze's only major-festival main-prize.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 53rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Patrice Chéreau's Silver Bear for Best Director for His Brother / Son frère — same-director Berlin Golden Bear → Best Director cross-ceremony pair (cer 2001 Intimacy → cer 2003 His Brother), within two years.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 53rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Sam Rockwell's Silver Bear for Best Actor for Confessions of a Dangerous Mind — George Clooney's directorial debut, biopic of Chuck Barris. Cer 2003 Berlin is Rockwell's first major-festival lead-acting prize; he later won the 2018 Best Supporting Actor Oscar for Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 53rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Famous THREE-WAY TIE for Silver Bear Best Actress: Meryl Streep + Nicole Kidman + Julianne Moore, all for the same film — Stephen Daldry's The Hours (Virginia Woolf-themed cross-era ensemble drama). Scraper produces 3 winner rows (split_co_recipients=true; expectations.json has a cer-2003 era_override with typical_winners=3). One of the most-cited multi-winner acting prizes in modern Berlin history. At the same ceremony Nicole Kidman went on to win the 2003 Best Actress Oscar for the same role — making her cer 2003 Berlin one-third of a Berlinale-Oscar overlap.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 53rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Chinese director Yang Li (Li Yang) for Blind Shaft — coal-mine murder thriller. Unusual OAC citation: directorial citation rather than a specific craft (no parenthetical specialty in the wikitext bullet).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 53rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2003.notes: 53rd Berlinale — famous THREE-WAY TIE for Best Actress (Streep + Kidman + Moore for The Hours), one of the most-cited multi-winner acting prizes in modern Berlin history (Kidman went on to take the same year's Best Actress Oscar for the same role). Patrice Chéreau wins Best Director two years after his cer 2001 Golden Bear. Alfred Bauer Prize is ABSENT from the cer 2003 Wikipedia article (article completeness gap, suppressed by expectations.json era_override; no MISSING REVIEW). Best Screenplay and BFF lifecycle-absent.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Turkish-German director Fatih Akın's Golden Bear for Head-On / Gegen die Wand — Turkish-German diaspora drama. First Turkish-German Golden Bear and a landmark for European Turkish-diasporic cinema. Akın's career-launching festival prize.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 54th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Argentine director Daniel Burman's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for Lost Embrace / El abrazo partido — same-film cross-honour: Lost Embrace also took Best Actor (Daniel Hendler) at the same ceremony.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 54th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: South Korean director Kim Ki-duk's Silver Bear for Best Director for Samaritan Girl. Kim followed up later that year with the Venice Silver Lion for 3-Iron — making 2004 his peak festival year (Berlin + Venice main-competition prizes within months).
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 54th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Uruguayan actor Daniel Hendler — same-film Grand Jury + Best Actor double for Lost Embrace at cer 2004.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 54th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: TIE for Silver Bear Best Actress: Catalina Sandino Moreno for Maria Full of Grace AND Charlize Theron for Monster (different films). Scraper produces 2 winner rows. Theron also won the 2004 Best Actress Oscar for Monster — same role as her share of this Berlin Silver Bear. Sandino Moreno was Oscar-nominated the same year — making cer 2004 Berlin a same-prize Oscar-overlap split between two simultaneously-Oscar-honoured performances.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 54th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded as 'Outstanding Artistic Achievement' (rare Berlin variant of OAC) to the FIFTEEN-PERSON ENSEMBLE CAST of the Swedish film Daybreak (Daggry / Daybreak): Leif Andrée + Pernilla August + Jan Coster + Jakob Eklund + Ingvar Hirdwall + Magnus Krepper + Johan Kvarnström + Camilla Larsson + Marika Lindström + Peter Lorentzon + Hampus Penttinen + Ann Petrén + Marie Richardson + Jenny Svärdsäter + Claes-Göran Turesson. Scraper produces a single row with all 15 names in the recipients_json (split_co_recipients=false on OAC). Largest-named-ensemble OAC in Berlin history.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 54th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2004.notes: 54th Berlinale — Fatih Akın's Turkish-German diasporic Golden Bear (Head-On); same-film Grand Jury + Best Actor double for Lost Embrace; Best Actress TIE (Sandino Moreno + Charlize Theron, both also 2004 Oscar-honoured); FIFTEEN-PERSON ENSEMBLE OAC for Daybreak (largest named OAC ensemble in Berlin history). Alfred Bauer Prize is ABSENT from the cer 2004 Wikipedia article (article gap, suppressed by expectations era_override). Best Screenplay and BFF lifecycle-absent.

berlin.golden_bear.note: South African director Mark Dornford-May's Golden Bear for U-Carmen eKhayelitsha — Xhosa-language reimagining of Bizet's Carmen, set in a Cape Town township. First South African Golden Bear and one of the most-unusual genre-source-material adaptations to win a major festival top prize. Permanent festival landmark.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 55th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Chinese director Gu Changwei's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for Peacock — Cultural Revolution-era family drama. Gu Changwei is best known as a cinematographer (Farewell My Concubine) — cer 2005 is his most-celebrated directorial prize.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 55th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: German director Marc Rothemund's Silver Bear for Best Director for Sophie Scholl – The Final Days — Nazi-resistance drama. Same-film cross-honour: Sophie Scholl also took Best Actress (Julia Jentsch) at the same ceremony — Best Director + Best Actress double, the strongest cer 2005 cross-honour.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 55th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Young American actor Lou Taylor Pucci — Mike Mills's debut feature Thumbsucker.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 55th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: German actress Julia Jentsch wins for Sophie Scholl – The Final Days — same-film Best Director + Best Actress double with Marc Rothemund's directing prize.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 55th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Taiwanese auteur Tsai Ming-liang for The Wayward Cloud — pornography-and-watermelon musical hybrid. Unusual OAC citation: directorial citation rather than a specific craft (no parenthetical specialty in the wikitext bullet, similar to cer 2003 Yang Li).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 55th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2005.notes: 55th Berlinale — South African Xhosa-language Golden Bear (U-Carmen eKhayelitsha, Mark Dornford-May); same-film Best Director + Best Actress double for Sophie Scholl – The Final Days; Tsai Ming-liang's only Berlinale main-prize. Alfred Bauer Prize is ABSENT from the cer 2005 Wikipedia article (article gap, suppressed by expectations era_override). Best Screenplay and BFF lifecycle-absent.

berlin.golden_bear.winner_film: Grbavica: The Land of My Dreams
berlin.golden_bear.note: Bosnian director Jasmila Žbanić's debut feature Grbavica takes the Golden Bear — wartime-rape-trauma drama set in postwar Sarajevo. Female-director debut Golden Bear win; one of the most-cited Berlinale political-cinema decisions of the mid-2000s. Žbanić served on the cer 2008 BFF jury the following year.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 56th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: TIE for Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize: Jafar Panahi for Offside AND Pernille Fischer Christensen for En soap. Scraper produces 2 winner rows (split as a TIE). The Panahi side is itself notable — Offside is about Iranian women smuggling themselves into a male-only football match; this Berlin Grand Jury was Panahi's first major-festival prize, predating his eventual Berlin Golden Bear (cer 2015). The En soap side sweeps a same-film cross-honour with Best First Feature at the same ceremony — same director (Pernille Fischer Christensen) wins Grand Jury + BFF for the same film.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 56th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Co-directors Michael Winterbottom AND Mat Whitecross take the Silver Bear for Best Director for The Road to Guantanamo — a docu-drama on the British detainees of Camp X-Ray. Scraper produces 2 winner rows (split_co_recipients=true on this category). Winterbottom is best known for 24 Hour Party People and Tristram Shandy; this is his Berlin peak.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 56th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: German actor Moritz Bleibtreu (Run Lola Run, Munich) wins Best Actor for the Houellebecq adaptation The Elementary Particles. One of the most-recognisable German actors of his generation receiving major-festival recognition.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 56th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: German actress Sandra Hüller's first major-festival acting prize for her debut feature Requiem (a 1970s Catholic-exorcism case). Hüller went on to become one of the most internationally-prized actresses of the 2020s — Cannes 2023 (Anatomy of a Fall + The Zone of Interest, both in competition the same year), 2024 Best Actress Oscar nomination, AMPAS member. Cer 2006 Best Actress is therefore Hüller's career launch.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 56th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: German actor-producer Jürgen Vogel — unusual OAC citation for an actor (most OAC awards go to crafts like cinematography or costume). Vogel was credited on The Free Will as actor, producer, AND co-writer; the OAC citation recognises his multi-hat auteur contribution. No specialty parenthetical in the wikitext bullet.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 56th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Argentine director Rodrigo Moreno — minimalist office-worker drama (El custodio). Moreno later returned to Berlinale and won an Encounters prize at cer 2023 for The Delinquents.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 56th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: INAUGURAL Best First Feature Award winner — cer 2006 (56th Berlinale) was the FIRST year this prize existed. Pernille Fischer Christensen's En soap also took a share of the Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize at the same ceremony — same-film, same-director debut-feature double of Grand Jury + BFF. Permanent festival landmark as the inaugural BFF laureate.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 56th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2006.notes: 56th Berlinale — INAUGURAL year of Best First Feature Award (Pernille Fischer Christensen / En soap, also taking a share of Grand Jury); Golden Bear to Jasmila Žbanić's debut Grbavica (rare female-director debut Golden Bear); Sandra Hüller's career-launching Best Actress for debut feature Requiem (years before her 2024 Oscar arc); Best Director TIE (Winterbottom + Whitecross); Grand Jury TIE (Panahi + Fischer Christensen). Best Screenplay was NOT awarded — Berlin had not yet introduced a dedicated Best Screenplay Silver Bear (debuted cer 2008); first_year=2008 in expectations means Phase D correctly skips this category.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Chinese director Wang Quan'an's Golden Bear for Tuya's Marriage — Inner Mongolia-set rural drama. Wang Quan'an returned to Berlin's prize roster three years later (cer 2010 Best Screenplay for Apart Together) — same-director cross-ceremony pair within four years.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 57th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Argentine director Ariel Rotter — same-film cross-honour with Best Actor: The Other / El otro also took Best Actor (Julio Chávez) at the same ceremony.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 57th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Israeli director Joseph Cedar's Silver Bear for Best Director — Beaufort follows the IDF's withdrawal from Lebanon. Cedar's most-decorated festival peak; Beaufort was nominated for the 2008 Best Foreign Language Film Oscar.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 57th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Argentine actor Julio Chávez — same-film Grand Jury + Best Actor double for The Other (with director Ariel Rotter on the Grand Jury side).
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 57th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: German actress Nina Hoss wins Best Actress for Christian Petzold's Yella — the first major Petzold-Hoss collaboration recognised by Berlin (the duo went on to make Phoenix, Barbara, etc.). Petzold won Best Director for Barbara five years later (cer 2012, also starring Hoss) — same-collaboration prize-pair across two ceremonies.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 57th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.winner_film: I'm a Cyborg, But That's OK
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: South Korean director Park Chan-wook — best known for Oldboy and the Vengeance trilogy — takes the Alfred Bauer Prize for I'm a Cyborg, But That's OK (a stylistic departure into psychiatric-ward romance). Park later won Cannes Best Director (2022) for Decision to Leave; cer 2007 Berlin remains his only Berlinale main-prize.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 57th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2007.notes: 57th Berlinale — same-film cross-honour for The Other (Grand Jury + Best Actor: Ariel Rotter + Julio Chávez); Park Chan-wook's only Berlinale main-prize. Best Screenplay NOT awarded (pre-cer-2008 lifecycle gap; expectations first_year=2008). Best First Feature winner ABSENT from Wikipedia article (article completeness gap; only the jury section appears). Outstanding Artistic Contribution awarded as 'For the Ensemble Cast of The Good Shepherd' (no individual recipient extractable; scraper correctly skips the row).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Brazilian director José Padilha's Golden Bear for Tropa de Elite / Elite Squad — Rio de Janeiro paramilitary-police thriller. The film became one of the most-influential Brazilian films of the 2000s and spawned a sequel; Padilha later directed RoboCop (2014) and the Narcos series.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 58th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: American documentarian Errol Morris's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for Standard Operating Procedure — Abu Ghraib torture-photography investigation. Morris is one of the most celebrated American documentarians (The Thin Blue Line, The Fog of War); cer 2008 Berlin is his only major fiction-festival main-prize.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 58th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Paul Thomas Anderson's Silver Bear for Best Director for There Will Be Blood — the most-celebrated American film at Berlin in the late 2000s. Same-film cross-honour: There Will Be Blood also took Outstanding Artistic Contribution (Jonny Greenwood for the score) at the same ceremony. PT Anderson had previously won the cer 2000 Berlin Golden Bear for Magnolia, making cer 2008 his second Berlin main-competition Silver Bear within the same decade.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 58th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Iranian actor Reza Naji wins Best Actor for Majid Majidi's The Song of Sparrows. Naji's win comes amid the Berlinale's continued recognition of Iranian cinema in the late 2000s.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 58th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: British actress Sally Hawkins's career-launching Best Actress for Mike Leigh's Happy-Go-Lucky. Hawkins went on to be Oscar-nominated for The Shape of Water (2017) and Blue Jasmine (2013). Cer 2008 is her major-festival breakthrough.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 58th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: INAUGURAL Best Screenplay Silver Bear winner — cer 2008 (58th Berlinale) was the FIRST year this prize existed at Berlin. Chinese director-writer Wang Xiaoshuai for In Love We Trust. Permanent festival landmark as the inaugural Berlin Best Screenplay laureate. Wang Xiaoshuai returned to Berlin's prize roster eleven years later (cer 2019 same-film acting double for So Long, My Son) — directorial cross-ceremony pair.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 58th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for the score of There Will Be Blood. Radiohead's Jonny Greenwood — better known internationally as a rock musician than a film composer — receives Berlin's craft-prize Silver Bear for what would become one of the most-celebrated American film scores of the 21st century. Same-film cross-honour with Best Director (Paul Thomas Anderson) at the same ceremony.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 58th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Mexican director Fernando Eimbcke — second feature after debut Duck Season. Eimbcke went on to chair the cer 2015 Best First Feature jury at Berlinale.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 58th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2008.notes: 58th Berlinale — INAUGURAL Best Screenplay Silver Bear (Wang Xiaoshuai for In Love We Trust). Same-film Best Director + OAC double for There Will Be Blood (Paul Thomas Anderson + Jonny Greenwood). PT Anderson collects his second Berlin main-competition prize within the same decade (Magnolia Golden Bear cer 2000 + this Best Director cer 2008). Sally Hawkins's career-launching Best Actress. Best First Feature winner ABSENT from Wikipedia article (article completeness gap; only the jury section appears).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Peruvian director Claudia Llosa's Golden Bear for The Milk of Sorrow / La teta asustada — Quechua-language drama on intergenerational trauma after the Shining Path conflict. First Peruvian Golden Bear; female-director second feature win. The film went on to a 2010 Best Foreign Language Film Oscar nomination.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 59th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: TIE for Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize: Adrián Biniez for Giant / Gigante AND Maren Ade for Everyone Else. Scraper produces 2 winner rows. The Maren Ade side is especially notable in retrospect: Everyone Else is the second feature of the future-Toni-Erdmann director, and Berlin recognised her seven years before her Cannes 2016 breakout. The Adrián Biniez side ALSO won the Alfred Bauer Prize at the same ceremony — same director, same film, two cross-category prizes.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 59th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Asghar Farhadi's first Berlin Silver Bear (Best Director for About Elly / Darbareye Elly) — pre-figures his cer 2011 Golden Bear for A Separation. Two consecutive Berlinale main prizes within three years: 2009 Best Director → 2011 Golden Bear. Cer 2009 launches Farhadi's international Berlinale arc.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 59th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Burkinabé-Malian actor Sotigui Kouyaté wins Best Actor for Rachid Bouchareb's London River. Kouyaté is the first Sub-Saharan-African actor to win a Berlin Best Actor Silver Bear; he died one year later (April 2010). Permanent festival landmark.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 59th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Austrian actress Birgit Minichmayr — Best Actress for Maren Ade's Everyone Else. Same-film cross-honour: Everyone Else also took a share of Grand Jury (Maren Ade direction). Minichmayr's most-celebrated film role.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 59th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Co-writers Oren Moverman and Alessandro Camon — Iraq War casualty-notification drama. Scraper captures both names in a single row (split_co_recipients=false on Best Screenplay, but the JSON lists both as recipients). The Messenger went on to two Oscar nominations (Best Original Screenplay, Best Supporting Actor for Woody Harrelson).
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 59th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for sound design of Katalin Varga (Peter Strickland's debut feature) — three-person sound team (György Kovács + Gábor ifj. Erdélyi + Tamás Székely; scraper merges into a single recipient row). Strickland later went on to direct Berberian Sound Studio and The Duke of Burgundy.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 59th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: TIE for Alfred Bauer Prize: Polish veteran Andrzej Wajda for Tatarak / Sweet Rush AND Argentine Adrián Biniez for Giant. Scraper produces 2 winner rows. The Biniez side is the FIRST recorded cer-same-festival double prize within this batch — Biniez ALSO took a share of the Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize at the same ceremony for the same film. Same director winning two distinct Silver Bears at one ceremony is extremely rare.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 59th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2009.notes: 59th Berlinale — Claudia Llosa's Peruvian Golden Bear (first Peruvian top prize, launching toward a 2010 Oscar nomination); Asghar Farhadi's first Berlin prize launches his Berlinale arc to the cer 2011 Golden Bear; Sotigui Kouyaté is the first Sub-Saharan-African Best Actor; Adrián Biniez wins TWO cer-2009 Silver Bears (Grand Jury TIE + Alfred Bauer TIE) for Giant — extraordinarily rare same-director, same-film, same-ceremony double-laureate. Best First Feature winner ABSENT from Wikipedia article (article completeness gap).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Turkish director Semih Kaplanoğlu's Golden Bear for Bal / Honey — third and final installment of his Yusuf trilogy (Egg / Milk / Honey, working backward through a poet's childhood). First Turkish Golden Bear; rare top-prize win for a third-of-a-trilogy completion film.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 60th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.winner_film: If I Want to Whistle, I Whistle
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Romanian director Florin Șerban's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for If I Want to Whistle, I Whistle. Same-film cross-honour: also took the Alfred Bauer Prize at the same ceremony — same-film, same-director Grand Jury + Alfred Bauer double.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 60th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Roman Polanski's Silver Bear for Best Director for The Ghost Writer — Polanski was under Swiss house arrest at the time of the ceremony following his US sex-crime extradition request, and accepted in absentia. One of the most controversial Berlin Silver Bears of the modern era for the off-screen circumstances. The film itself is a Tony Blair stand-in political thriller.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 60th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: TIE for Silver Bear Best Actor — Russian actors Grigoriy Dobrygin AND Sergei Puskepalis for How I Ended This Summer (an Arctic-research-station two-hander). Scraper produces 2 winner rows (split_co_recipients=true on this category). Same-film TRIPLE cross-honour at cer 2010: How I Ended This Summer also took Outstanding Artistic Contribution (Cinematography, Pavel Kostomarov) — Best Actor TIE + OAC for one film, only Best Director and Golden Bear evaded.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 60th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Japanese actress Shinobu Terajima — anti-war drama about a quadruple-amputee Imperial Japanese Army veteran returning home. The film and performance directly engage with Japan's wartime memory.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 60th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Co-writers Jin Na and Wang Quan'an. Wang Quan'an returns to Berlin's prize roster three years after his cer 2007 Golden Bear (Tuya's Marriage) — same-director cross-ceremony pair within four years.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 60th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for Camera (cinematography) of How I Ended This Summer. Same-film cross-honour with the Best Actor TIE at the same ceremony — How I Ended This Summer takes a triple-prize sweep (Best Actor TIE + OAC) on the strength of its remote Arctic-station production.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 60th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.winner_film: If I Want to Whistle, I Whistle
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Romanian director Florin Șerban — same-film, same-director Alfred Bauer + Grand Jury double for If I Want to Whistle, I Whistle. Two prizes for one debut feature at the same ceremony.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 60th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2010.notes: 60th Berlinale (anniversary edition) — Turkish first Golden Bear (Semih Kaplanoğlu, Bal — third of Yusuf trilogy); Roman Polanski's in-absentia Best Director Silver Bear under Swiss house arrest; If I Want to Whistle, I Whistle takes a same-film Grand Jury + Alfred Bauer double (Florin Șerban); How I Ended This Summer takes a same-film Best Actor TIE + OAC double (Dobrygin + Puskepalis + Pavel Kostomarov cinematography). Best First Feature winner ABSENT from Wikipedia article (article completeness gap).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Asghar Farhadi's Golden Bear for Nader and Simin, A Separation — the most-decorated single Berlin film in modern history. The film also took the Best Actor + Best Actress ensemble awards at the same ceremony (the entire male and female casts of A Separation), and went on to win the 2012 Best Foreign Language Film Oscar. First Iranian Golden Bear since the 1980s; cer 2011 launches Iranian arthouse cinema's most internationally visible decade.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 61st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Hungarian master Béla Tarr's final feature The Turin Horse takes the Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize. Tarr publicly declared after this ceremony that The Turin Horse would be his last film — Berlin therefore holds the laureate role for his retirement-feature.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 61st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: German Berlin School director Ulrich Köhler — Cameroon-set medical drama.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 61st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: American director Joshua Marston (with Andamion Murataj) — Albanian blood-feud drama. Marston's follow-up to Maria Full of Grace.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 61st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: TWO Outstanding Artistic Contribution Silver Bears awarded at cer 2011, both for The Prize: Barbara Enriquez for Production Design + Wojciech Staroń for Camera (cinematography). Same-film double-craft OAC is unusual — Berlin recognised distinct craft contributions on the same film as separate prizes. Scraper produces 2 winner rows accordingly.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 61st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: German director Andres Veiel — RAF / Red Army Faction-era drama.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 61st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2011.notes: 61st Berlinale — A Separation sweeps Golden Bear + Best Actor (ENSEMBLE) + Best Actress (ENSEMBLE), launching toward a 2012 Best Foreign Language Film Oscar. Best Actor and Best Actress were awarded as ENSEMBLE prizes (the entire male/female casts of A Separation) — scraper correctly skips these rows because no individual recipient is named in the wikitext bullet ('the actor ensemble of' / 'the actress ensemble of' triggers the NON_PERSON_NAMES filter). Both prizes were genuinely awarded; just not extractable as individual-recipient rows. Best First Feature Award is ABSENT from this ceremony's Wikipedia article (article completeness gap — only the jury list appears, not the winner). Two OAC Silver Bears awarded for The Prize (Production Design + Camera). Béla Tarr's final feature wins Grand Jury.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Italian veteran brother-directors Paolo and Vittorio Taviani take the Golden Bear for Caesar Must Die — a docu-fictional staging of Shakespeare's Julius Caesar performed by inmates of Rebibbia prison in Rome. The Tavianis (born 1929 + 1931) were among the oldest Golden Bear laureates in festival history when they won. Vittorio died in 2018; Paolo continued directing into his 90s.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 62nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Hungarian director Benedek Fliegauf — drama based on real Roma family killings in Hungary.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 62nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Christian Petzold's first Silver Bear for Best Director — East German Cold War drama starring Nina Hoss. Petzold returns to the Berlin prize roster eleven years later (cer 2023 Grand Jury for Afire).
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 62nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Danish actor Mikkel Boe Følsgaard — debut-feature performer winning Best Actor at his graduation from Copenhagen's National School of Theatre. Same-film cross-honour: A Royal Affair also took Best Screenplay.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 62nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Congolese first-time actress Rachel Mwanza — discovered on the streets of Kinshasa for Kim Nguyen's child-soldier drama Rebelle / War Witch. Among the youngest Berlin Best Actress laureates and one of very few Best Actress winners with no prior acting credits.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 62nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Danish writer-director Nikolaj Arcel (with Rasmus Heisterberg) — same-film cross-honour with Best Actor at cer 2012.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 62nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for cinematography of White Deer Plain (Wang Quan'an).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 62nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Portuguese director Miguel Gomes — Tabu is a black-and-white silent-cinema-style colonial-Mozambique romance. Gomes's most-decorated festival peak; he later took Cannes Best Director (2024) for Grand Tour.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 62nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2012.notes: 62nd Berlinale — Taviani brothers (born 1929 + 1931) take a late-career Golden Bear for prison-Shakespeare adaptation. A Royal Affair takes a same-film Best Actor + Best Screenplay double. Best First Feature Award is ABSENT from this ceremony's Wikipedia article (article completeness gap).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Romanian director Călin Peter Netzer's Golden Bear for Child's Pose — keeping the Berlinale's mid-2010s Romanian New Wave streak. Netzer's editor Dana Bunescu later won OAC for editing (cer 2017 Ana, mon amour) under the same director — a same-collaboration prize-pair across two ceremonies.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 63rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.winner_film: An Episode in the Life of an Iron Picker
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Bosnian director Danis Tanović's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for An Episode in the Life of an Iron Picker — same-film cross-honour with Best Actor (Nazif Mujić, the real Roma scrap-metal collector who plays himself). Tanović returns to the Berlinale prize roster three years later (cer 2016 Grand Jury for Death in Sarajevo) — two Grand Jury Prizes within four years.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 63rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: American director David Gordon Green's Silver Bear for Best Director — Prince Avalanche is a Paul Rudd / Emile Hirsch two-hander shot during a personal-project pivot from his stoner-comedy phase. Green's only major festival win.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 63rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.winner_film: An Episode in the Life of an Iron Picker
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Roma scrap-metal collector Nazif Mujić — first non-professional actor to win Berlin Best Actor in the modern era, playing a fictionalised version of himself in a Tanović docu-drama. Mujić died of a heart attack in 2018, partly attributed to ongoing poverty after the festival despite his Silver Bear.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 63rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Chilean actress Paulina García — late-career Best Actress for Sebastián Lelio's Gloria. Lelio returned to Berlin and won Best Screenplay four years later (cer 2017, A Fantastic Woman) — same-director-different-collaborator cross-ceremony pair.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 63rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Iranian director Jafar Panahi's Best Screenplay for Closed Curtain — written and made under his post-2010 Iranian filmmaking ban. Panahi could not attend; Berlin awarded the prize as an act of solidarity. Panahi later won the Golden Bear at cer 2015 (Taxi) — a Berlin three-prize-window arc (cer 2010 in absentia for Offside, cer 2013 Best Screenplay, cer 2015 Golden Bear).
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 63rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded to Aziz Zhambakiyev for Harmony Lessons (Emir Baigazin) — Kazakh school-drama. No specialty noted in the wikitext bullet (unusual omission for OAC).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 63rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.winner_film: Vic and Flo Saw a Bear
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Quebec director Denis Côté — Côté returns to Berlin's main competition repeatedly through the 2010s-2020s; cer 2013 Alfred Bauer is his only main-prize win.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 63rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2013.notes: 63rd Berlinale — same-film Grand Jury + Best Actor double for An Episode in the Life of an Iron Picker (Tanović + non-professional actor Nazif Mujić). Jafar Panahi takes Best Screenplay in absentia under his Iranian filmmaking ban — Berlinale's continued Panahi-solidarity arc. Best First Feature Award is ABSENT from this ceremony's Wikipedia article (article completeness gap).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Chinese director Diao Yinan's Golden Bear for Black Coal, Thin Ice — neo-noir thriller. Same-film cross-honour: Black Coal, Thin Ice also took Best Actor (Liao Fan) at the same ceremony — Golden Bear + Best Actor double, the strongest cross-honour pattern of cer 2014.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Wes Anderson's first major festival prize — Grand Jury for The Grand Budapest Hotel, the festival's opening film. Pre-figures Anderson's Berlin Best Director Silver Bear four years later (cer 2018, Isle of Dogs). The Grand Budapest Hotel went on to win four Oscars.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Richard Linklater's Silver Bear for Best Director for Boyhood — the 12-year-shot coming-of-age epic. Boyhood became one of the most-celebrated American films of the 2010s; Berlin's Best Director Silver Bear was its first major festival recognition. Linklater later went on to win at cer 2025 (Andrew Scott Best Supporting Performance for Blue Moon, a Linklater-directed Lorenz Hart biopic) — same-director Berlinale-recognition cross-decade pair.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Chinese actor Liao Fan wins for Black Coal, Thin Ice — same-film Golden Bear + Best Actor double. Liao Fan and Diao Yinan reunited and went on to make further neo-noir collaborations.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Japanese actress Haru Kuroki wins for Yōji Yamada's The Little House — period-domestic-melodrama lead. Yamada is best known for the Tora-san film series.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: German writer-director Dietrich Brüggemann (with Anna Brüggemann) — Catholic-fundamentalism family drama structured in 14 long static-shot scenes echoing the Stations of the Cross.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for Cinematography of Blind Massage (Lou Ye). Unusual citation — the bullet credits the film and its director (Lou Ye) rather than a named DP, possibly because the prize honoured the film's cinematography as a whole.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Alain Resnais's final feature Life of Riley takes the Alfred Bauer Prize. Resnais (born 1922) died one month after this Berlinale, on 1 March 2014, making cer 2014 a posthumous-coda festival appearance for one of European cinema's greatest auteurs.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Mexican director Alonso Ruizpalacios's debut Güeros — black-and-white road-trip drama. Ruizpalacios returned to Berlin and won Best Screenplay four years later (cer 2018, Museum) and OAC seven years later (cer 2021, A Cop Movie editing) — three Berlinale prizes across three different categories.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 64th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2014.notes: 64th Berlinale — Black Coal, Thin Ice takes Golden Bear + Best Actor double; Wes Anderson's first festival prize (Grand Jury for The Grand Budapest Hotel) pre-figures his cer 2018 Best Director Silver Bear; Richard Linklater's Boyhood is launched on the European festival circuit; Alain Resnais wins his final festival prize one month before death. Alonso Ruizpalacios's BFF debut here precedes two further Berlinale prizes.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Iranian director Jafar Panahi's Golden Bear for Taxi — shot secretly in defiance of his Iranian filmmaking ban; Panahi could not attend the ceremony and the prize was accepted by his niece. Cer 2015 completes Panahi's Berlinale three-prize arc (cer 2010 in absentia for Offside; cer 2013 Best Screenplay for Closed Curtain; cer 2015 Golden Bear for Taxi). Berlin's most-sustained festival-solidarity statement of the modern era.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Chilean director Pablo Larraín's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for The Club — secluded-priest sex-abuse drama. Larraín emerges in the mid-2010s as one of the most internationally-prized Latin American directors; he goes on to make Jackie (2016), Spencer (2021), and Maria (2024).
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: TIE for Best Director at cer 2015: Radu Jude for Aferim! (Romanian historical drama on 19th-century Roma slavery) AND Małgorzata Szumowska for Body. Scraper produces 2 separate winner rows (split_co_recipients=true on this category). Notable directorial-tie ceremony; Jude returns to Berlin's prize roster six years later with the cer 2021 Golden Bear for Bad Luck Banging or Loony Porn — same-festival prize-pair across two ceremonies. Szumowska had previously won the cer 2013 Alfred Bauer Prize and won the cer 2018 Silver Bear Grand Jury.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: British actor Tom Courtenay wins for Andrew Haigh's 45 Years. Same-film cross-honour: 45 Years also took Best Actress (Charlotte Rampling) — third recorded same-film Best Actor + Best Actress double in this batch window (cer 2011 A Separation ensemble; cer 2015 45 Years; cer 2019 So Long, My Son).
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: British actress Charlotte Rampling wins for 45 Years — long-overdue major-festival acting Silver Bear after a six-decade career. Same-film double with co-star Tom Courtenay's Best Actor.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Chilean documentarian Patricio Guzmán's Best Screenplay for The Pearl Button — second installment of his cosmic-meditation trilogy on Chile's geography, politics, and history (after Nostalgia for the Light, before The Cordillera of Dreams). Documentary winning a Best Screenplay Silver Bear is highly unusual.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: TWO Outstanding Artistic Contribution Silver Bears awarded at cer 2015 in a TIE for cinematography: Sturla Brandth Grøvlen for Victoria (the famous one-take Berlin-set heist drama, two and a half hours, ONE continuous shot) AND Sergey Mikhalchuk + Evgeniy Privin for Under Electric Clouds. Scraper produces 2 winner rows. Victoria's one-shot achievement is one of the most cited recent Berlin OAC-cinematography wins.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Guatemalan director Jayro Bustamante's debut Ixcanul — Mayan Kaqchikel-language drama. Bustamante was on the cer 2017 BFF jury, signalling Berlinale's continued investment in Latin American debut features.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Mexican director Gabriel Ripstein's debut — son of legendary Mexican director Arturo Ripstein.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 65th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2015.notes: 65th Berlinale — Jafar Panahi's secret-shot Taxi takes the Golden Bear (in absentia), completing his Berlinale three-prize solidarity arc (2010 → 2013 → 2015). Best Director TIE (Radu Jude + Małgorzata Szumowska); 45 Years takes a same-film Best Actor + Best Actress double (Tom Courtenay + Charlotte Rampling); OAC TIE for cinematography (Victoria one-shot achievement + Under Electric Clouds).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Documentary Golden Bear for Gianfranco Rosi's Fuocoammare / Fire at Sea — observational documentary about migrant rescue operations on the Italian island of Lampedusa. Rosi had previously won Venice's Golden Lion for documentary Sacro GRA in 2013, making him one of only a handful of directors to take top prizes at both Berlin and Venice for documentary work. The 2016 win marked the second documentary Golden Bear of the modern era and a continued Berlinale political-documentary streak.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Bosnian director Danis Tanović — Oscar winner for No Man's Land (2001) — takes the Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for Death in Sarajevo, set on the centennial of the Archduke Franz Ferdinand assassination.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Mia Hansen-Løve's first Silver Bear for Best Director — French auteur honoured for the Isabelle Huppert vehicle Things to Come (L'Avenir). Hansen-Løve emerges as one of the most consistently prized European directors of the late 2010s.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Tunisian actor Majd Mastoura — first-time performer recognised for his role in Hedi (Mohamed Ben Attia's debut feature). Same-film cross-honour: Hedi also won Best First Feature at the same ceremony.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Danish actress Trine Dyrholm wins for Thomas Vinterberg's The Commune — recognised for her layered portrayal of a wife whose marriage is upended by her husband's open-marriage proposal in a 1970s Copenhagen commune.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for cinematography of Crosscurrent. Mark Lee Ping Bin (Mark Lee Ping Bing / Lee Ping-bin) is one of Asian cinema's most celebrated DPs — long-time collaborator of Hou Hsiao-hsien and the cinematographer of In the Mood for Love (uncredited 2nd-unit) and Café Lumière.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.winner_film: A Lullaby to the Sorrowful Mystery
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Filipino slow-cinema auteur Lav Diaz takes the Alfred Bauer Prize for his eight-hour A Lullaby to the Sorrowful Mystery — one of the longest films ever to play in any festival main competition. Diaz went on to win the Venice Golden Lion later the same year (2016) for The Woman Who Left, making 2016 his international breakout.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Tunisian director Mohamed Ben Attia's debut Hedi sweeps a Best First Feature + Best Actor double at cer 2016 — same-film, same-festival cross-category honour. Berlin's stamp helped propel the new wave of Tunisian arthouse cinema in the mid-2010s.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 66th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2016.notes: 66th Berlinale — second-ever modern documentary Golden Bear (Gianfranco Rosi). Best Screenplay was NOT awarded at this ceremony (real-data gap, scraper correctly returns 0 rows; Phase D tolerance covers). Hedi takes a same-film Best Actor + Best First Feature double; Lav Diaz wins Alfred Bauer with an eight-hour film and goes on to take the Venice Golden Lion later in 2016.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Hungarian director Ildikó Enyedi's first Golden Bear — On Body and Soul is a slaughterhouse-set romance featuring shared deer dreams. The film went on to a 2018 Best Foreign Language Film Oscar nomination, making 2017 Enyedi's international breakout after a quarter-century gap from her 1989 Camera d'Or win for My Twentieth Century.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Senegalese-French director Alain Gomis — Kinshasa-set music drama.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Finnish auteur Aki Kaurismäki's Silver Bear for Best Director — second installment of his Refugee/Port trilogy. One of Kaurismäki's last announced features before his post-2017 'retirement' that he subsequently reversed.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Austrian actor Georg Friedrich — long-time character actor finally winning a major festival prize for his lead role in Thomas Arslan's Bright Nights.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.winner_film: On the Beach at Night Alone
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: South Korean actress Kim Min-hee wins for Hong Sang-soo's On the Beach at Night Alone — a thinly-veiled autobiographical film about her relationship with the (married) director, which had been a Korean tabloid scandal. Hong-Kim collaboration recognition that pre-figures Hong's three-Silver-Bear Berlinale streak in cer 2021-2024.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Sebastián Lelio (with Gonzalo Maza) takes Best Screenplay for A Fantastic Woman — film centred on a transgender protagonist (Daniela Vega). The film went on to win the 2018 Best Foreign Language Film Oscar, making cer 2017 Berlin its international launchpad.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for editing of Ana, mon amour (Călin Peter Netzer). Dana Bunescu is one of Romanian New Wave cinema's signature editors (4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days; Aurora; Toni Erdmann).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Polish veteran Agnieszka Holland (Europa Europa, In Darkness) takes Alfred Bauer for Spoor — eco-mystery thriller adapted from Olga Tokarczuk's novel.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Carla Simón's debut feature Estiu 1993 (Summer 1993) wins Best First Feature — Catalan-language autobiographical film. Simón returned to Berlin five years later (cer 2022) and won the Golden Bear for Alcarràs, completing a Berlin BFF → Golden Bear arc within the same festival.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 67th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2017.notes: 67th Berlinale — Ildikó Enyedi returns after a 25-year hiatus; Hong Sang-soo / Kim Min-hee Berlinale connection that pre-figures Hong's later Silver Bear streak; A Fantastic Woman launches toward an Oscar. Carla Simón's BFF win here precedes her cer 2022 Golden Bear, making her a rare both-Berlin-debut-and-top-prize winner.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Adina Pintilie's debut feature Touch Me Not wins the Golden Bear AND Best First Feature at the same ceremony — extraordinarily rare same-film, same-director, same-festival double. Touch Me Not is a documentary-fiction hybrid about intimacy and disability. Pintilie joins the very short list of directors who won the Berlinale's top prize on a debut feature.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Polish director Małgorzata Szumowska's second Berlin Silver Bear (after her 2015 Best Director win for Body), confirming her as a Berlinale regular in the 2010s.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Wes Anderson's first major festival award for direction — Isle of Dogs is his second stop-motion animated feature. Notable that a stop-motion film took a competitive director Silver Bear; the Berlinale opened with Isle of Dogs that year.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Young French actor Anthony Bajon — recovery-drama lead role.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Paraguayan actress Ana Brun — late-career performer winning a major festival prize. The Heiresses takes an unusual same-film double at cer 2018, also winning the Alfred Bauer Prize for director Marcelo Martinessi.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Mexican director-writer Alonso Ruizpalacios (with Manuel Alcalá) — Museum is a heist film starring Gael García Bernal. Ruizpalacios returns to Berlin's prize roster years later (cer 2021 OAC for editing of A Cop Movie).
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for costume and production design of Dovlatov (Aleksey German Jr.). Unusual dual-craft OAC citation — Berlin recognises both costume and production design as a unified contribution.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: Paraguayan director Marcelo Martinessi — The Heiresses takes Alfred Bauer for director AND Best Actress (Ana Brun) at the same ceremony. Cer 2018 has TWO same-film cross-category doubles (Touch Me Not for Golden Bear + BFF; The Heiresses for Best Actress + Alfred Bauer) — the most concentrated cross-honour year in this batch.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Touch Me Not's second prize at cer 2018 — same-film, same-director Golden Bear + Best First Feature double. Permanent festival landmark.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 68th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2018.notes: 68th Berlinale — landmark cross-honour ceremony. Touch Me Not (Adina Pintilie debut) takes Golden Bear + Best First Feature; The Heiresses (Marcelo Martinessi debut) takes Best Actress (Ana Brun) + Alfred Bauer. Wes Anderson's first festival Best Director Silver Bear.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Israeli director Nadav Lapid's Golden Bear for Synonyms — autobiographical Paris-set drama about an Israeli emigrant. Lapid went on to a controversial 2022 IFFI Goa jury president speech criticising The Kashmir Files; cer 2019 Berlin remains his major-festival peak.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: François Ozon's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize for By the Grace of God (Grâce à Dieu) — based on real Catholic-Church abuse cases in Lyon. Ozon's first major Berlin prize after a long career.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Berlin School director Angela Schanelec's first Silver Bear for Best Director — her formal-cinema return. Schanelec returns four years later for Best Screenplay (cer 2023, Music) — two distinct Silver Bears in this batch window.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Chinese actor Wang Jingchun wins for Wang Xiaoshuai's So Long, My Son. Same-film acting double at cer 2019 — co-star Yong Mei takes Best Actress. The last Berlinale before the COVID pandemic and the last cer with the gendered Best Actor + Best Actress structure paired on the same film.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: Chinese actress Yong Mei wins for the same film (So Long, My Son) as her Best Actor co-star Wang Jingchun — historic same-film cross-acting double. One of the very few Berlinales where a single film took both gendered acting Silver Bears.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Co-writer pair: Italian director Claudio Giovannesi and journalist-author Roberto Saviano (with Maurizio Braucci, captured by scraper as primary pair). Saviano is best known internationally for Gomorrah (2008 book + film). Piranhas is a Naples youth-mafia drama.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for Cinematography of Out Stealing Horses (Hans Petter Moland; Stellan Skarsgård lead) — Norwegian-Swedish-Danish co-production.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.note: German director Nora Fingscheidt's debut System Crasher takes the LAST Alfred Bauer Prize before its retirement — the prize was discontinued in 2020 following revelations about Bauer's Nazi-era ties. Fingscheidt's win therefore holds permanent landmark status as the FINAL Alfred Bauer laureate.
berlin.alfred_bauer_prize.source: 69th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2019.notes: 69th Berlinale — last pre-COVID festival. So Long, My Son takes a same-film Best Actor + Best Actress double (Wang Jingchun + Yong Mei). System Crasher (Nora Fingscheidt) is the FINAL Alfred Bauer laureate before the prize's retirement. Best First Feature was NOT awarded at this ceremony per the Wikipedia article (real-data gap, scraper correctly returns 0 rows; Phase D tolerance covers).

berlin.golden_bear.note: Iranian director Mohammad Rasoulof's Golden Bear for There Is No Evil — a four-part anthology examining capital punishment in Iran. Rasoulof was BANNED from leaving Iran by the regime and could not attend the ceremony; the prize was accepted in absentia. Rasoulof later went into exile and won the 2024 Cannes Special Jury Prize for The Seed of the Sacred Fig — making the cer 2020 Golden Bear part of one of the most-defining political-cinema arcs in modern festival history.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: American director Eliza Hittman's Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize — abortion-access road-trip drama. The film's pre-pandemic festival run was abruptly truncated by the March 2020 COVID lockdown; the Berlin Silver Bear remained its highest-profile recognition.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Hong Sang-soo's first Silver Bear for Best Director — pre-figures his three-Silver-Bear Berlin streak across cer 2021-2024 (Best Screenplay 2021, Grand Jury 2022, Grand Jury 2024). Cer 2020 is the start of Hong's most-decorated Berlinale era.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.note: Italian actor Elio Germano — LAST winner of the gendered Silver Bear for Best Actor before its retirement. Berlin announced post-cer-2020 that the gendered acting prizes would be replaced by gender-neutral Best Leading Performance + Best Supporting Performance starting at cer 2021. Germano therefore holds permanent landmark status as the FINAL Best Actor laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actor.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.note: German actress Paula Beer — LAST winner of the gendered Silver Bear for Best Actress, in Christian Petzold's Undine (the first installment of his Petzold-Beer water-mythology series; Petzold returned to Berlin's prize roster in cer 2023 with Afire taking the Grand Jury Prize). Beer holds permanent landmark status as the FINAL Best Actress laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_actress.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Italian D'Innocenzo brothers — captured as a single recipient by the scraper (split_co_recipients=false on Best Screenplay).
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for cinematography of DAU. Natasha — installment of Ilya Khrzhanovsky's controversial DAU project (decade-long immersive Soviet-research-institute reconstruction). German DP Jürgen Jürges (long-time Fassbinder and Haneke collaborator) recognised for shooting under the project's notoriously unconventional production conditions.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.note: Colombian-French director Camilo Restrepo's experimental debut.
berlin.best_first_feature.source: 70th Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2020.notes: 70th Berlinale — final pre-pandemic in-person festival. THREE simultaneous lifecycle landmarks: (1) Mohammad Rasoulof's in-absentia Golden Bear under Iranian travel ban; (2) Elio Germano is the FINAL Best Actor; (3) Paula Beer is the FINAL Best Actress. Alfred Bauer Prize is ABSENT from cer 2020 — retired after the cer 2019 ceremony following revelations about Bauer's Nazi-era ties. Hong Sang-soo's first Silver Bear, beginning his 2020-2024 Berlinale streak.

berlin.golden_bear.winner_film: Bad Luck Banging or Loony Porn
berlin.golden_bear.note: Romanian filmmaker Radu Jude's first Golden Bear, awarded at the 71st Berlinale's COVID-restricted online edition (industry-only March 2021; public summer 2021). Jude later won Berlin Best Screenplay for Kontinental '25 (cer 2025) — same-festival prize-pair across two ceremonies.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Ryusuke Hamaguchi's Berlin Grand Jury Prize for Wheel of Fortune and Fantasy. Hamaguchi went on to win the 2022 Best International Feature Oscar for Drive My Car, making 2021 his international breakout year.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.note: FIRST winner of the gender-neutral Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance, introduced at the 71st Berlinale (cer 2021) replacing the gendered Best Actor + Best Actress. Maren Eggert holds permanent landmark status as the inaugural Best Leading Performance laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.note: FIRST winner of the gender-neutral Silver Bear for Best Supporting Performance, introduced at the 71st Berlinale (cer 2021) alongside Best Leading Performance. Lilla Kizlinger holds permanent landmark status as the inaugural Best Supporting Performance laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Hong Sang-soo's Berlin Best Screenplay for Introduction. Hong is a Berlin perennial — he won the Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize the following year (cer 2022) for The Novelist's Film, and again in cer 2024 for A Traveler's Needs. Three consecutive Berlinale prizes in the 2021-2024 window is one of the most concentrated festival runs of any director.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Awarded for editing of A Cop Movie (Mexican docu-fiction hybrid by Alonso Ruizpalacios).
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 71st Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2021.notes: First COVID-restricted Berlinale, split across two-stage ceremony (industry March 2021, public summer 2021). First year of gender-neutral acting prizes. Alfred Bauer Prize newly retired (after 2019 revelations); not awarded cer 2021+. Best First Feature returned at the standard cadence.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Carla Simón's first Golden Bear — Catalan-language film by a Spanish female director. Alcarràs is a quiet family drama about a peach-farming family losing their land, marking a continued Berlinale preference for European art cinema with regional/agricultural settings.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Hong Sang-soo's second consecutive Berlinale prize (Best Screenplay 2021 → Grand Jury 2022) — same director winning two distinct main-competition Silver Bears in two years.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Claire Denis's first Silver Bear for Best Director — French auteur honoured for Both Sides of the Blade (Avec amour et acharnement) starring Juliette Binoche and Vincent Lindon.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.winner_film: Rabiye Kurnaz vs. George W. Bush
berlin.silver_bear_best_leading_performa.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.note: Indonesian actress Laura Basuki — Berlin's second Best Supporting Performance laureate.
berlin.silver_bear_best_supporting_perfo.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.winner_film: Rabiye Kurnaz vs. George W. Bush
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.note: Same-film double-laureate at cer 2022: Rabiye Kurnaz vs. George W. Bush won both Best Leading Performance (Meltem Kaptan) and Best Screenplay (Laila Stieler) — a same-ceremony cross-category honour.
berlin.silver_bear_best_screenplay.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.note: Cambodian director Rithy Panh (and collaborator Sarit Mang — scraper captures Panh as primary). Panh is best known for genocide-witness documentary work (The Missing Picture, Cannes 2013). Berlinale OAC awarded for the artistic contribution as a whole, not a single craft specialty.
berlin.silver_bear_outstanding_artistic_.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.best_first_feature.source: 72nd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

2022.notes: First post-COVID in-person Berlinale (held in February 2022 with reduced capacity). Hong Sang-soo's second consecutive Silver Bear; Carla Simón becomes a young female Golden Bear laureate; Rabiye Kurnaz takes a same-film double across acting + screenplay.

berlin.golden_bear.note: Documentary film wins the Golden Bear — On the Adamant is a French observational documentary set on a Seine river-boat psychiatric day-care centre. Documentary Golden Bears are rare; Nicolas Philibert (To Be and to Have, 2002) had previously been a celebrated documentarian without major festival hardware. The 2023 win signalled the Berlinale's continued openness to non-fiction in the main competition.
berlin.golden_bear.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.note: Christian Petzold's first Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize (after multiple Berlin nominations). Afire (Roter Himmel) is part of Petzold's elemental-themed trilogy with Paula Beer.
berlin.silver_bear_grand_jury.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article

berlin.silver_bear_best_director.note: Veteran French director Philippe Garrel's first Berlin Best Director (his fourth-decade festival recognition). The Plough is a family drama featuring three of Garrel's own children.
berlin.silver_bear_best_director.source: 73rd Berlin International Film Festival Wikipedia article
